rulings.Case Title: COUNTY OF SAN MATEO VS. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgmnent On The Pleadings;Memo P&A; Declaration; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 21, 2008, LINE 6 DEFENDANT CITY OF OAKLAND'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. THE LEGISLATURE HAS ADDRESSED THE RELIEF SOUGHT BY PLAINTIFF AND AS A CONSEQUENCE, THERE IS NO PRESENT CONTROVERSY ALLEGED. BECAUSE PLAINTIFF CONTENDS IN ITS OPPOSITION THAT ANOTHER ENTITY COULD TAKE ADVANTAGE OF THE CHALLENGED REGULATION, LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS A PENDING ADMINISTARTIVE ACTION TO OBTAIN THE REALLOCATION. PLAINTIFF MUST FIRST EXHAUST THIS ADMINISTARTIVE REMEDY. IF PLAINTIFF IS DISSATISFIED WITH THE ADMINISTARTIVE DETERMINATION, IT MAY THAN SEEK REVIEW BY WRIT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: WADE ANTHONY ROBERTSON VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 21, 2008, line 15, PETITIONER WADE ROBERTSON'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE PRIOR WRIT WAS BROUGHT UNDER CCP SECTION 1085, PLAINTIFF WAS SEEKING REVIEW OF A QUASI-JUDICIAL PROCEEDING. CCP 1094.5 IS THE APPROPRIATE FORM OF WRIT TO DO SO. IN CONSIDERING THE MERITS OF THE PRIOR WRIT, THE COURT NECESSARILY TREATED IT AS AN ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS WRIT, WHICH THE COURT MAY DO. COUNTY OF SAN DIEGO V. STATE OF CALIFORNIA (1997) 15 CAL.4TH 68, 109. THIS IS THE SECOND SUCH WRIT FROM THE UNDERLYING DMV HEARING. VEHICLE CODE SECTION 14400 GUARANTEES A RIGHT TO JUDICIAL REVIEW BUT THERE APPEARS TO BE NO AUTHORITY AUTHORIZING MULTIPLE WRITS BASED ON DIFFERENT ISSUES FROM THE SAME DMV HEARING. =(302/PJM/AY)
